title: episodic memory entry v0.8
description: Schema for one entry in a persona's append-only episodic memory log (memory/episodic.jsonl, one JSON object per line). v0.8 elevates the runtime memory-governance guarantees into the spec: every entry carries a provenance source and forms a tamper-evident hash chain (prev_hash -> hash). Deletion is tombstone semantics (a superseding entry is appended; the log is never rewritten), so removals remain auditable. This makes governed, poisoning-resistant memory portable across conforming runtimes, not specific to one implementation.

ts.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the entry was committed.

source.description: Provenance channel. Drives trust + sensitive-action gates: 'user' is most trusted; 'tool'/'synthesis' are the poisoning-prone channels and must not, on their own, justify a sensitive action.

tags.description: Free-form tags. Reserved: 'tombstone' (a deletion record; its 'target:<hash>' tag names the superseded entry) and 'injection-flagged' (written under a detected prompt injection).

prev_hash.description: Hash of the previous entry in the chain; empty string for the first entry. Lineage / tamper-evidence.

hash.description: sha256 over {ts, content, source, tags, prev_hash}. Any rewrite of a prior line breaks the chain and is detectable.
